name: Alternative 3 Documentary
description: The 'Alternative 3' documentary, originally aired in the UK as part of the 'Science Report' series on June 20, 1977, presented a sensational and controversial theory. It posited that a secret agreement existed between world powers to establish human colonies on Mars and other celestial bodies as a contingency plan for a perceived environmental catastrophe on Earth. The documentary claimed that prominent scientists and individuals had mysteriously disappeared, allegedly recruited or silenced due to their knowledge of the 'Alternative 3' project. It featured purported interviews, grainy footage, and coded messages to support its claims. The documentary was presented as a serious investigation, but its producers later revealed it was a hoax intended as an April Fool's Day joke that was broadcast late due to scheduling issues. Despite this admission, the 'Alternative 3' theory continues to be embraced by some as a genuine conspiracy, with proponents citing inconsistencies in the explanation and perceived evidence that aligns with the documentary's narrative. Mainstream scientific and academic perspectives universally reject the 'Alternative 3' claims, highlighting the lack of credible evidence, the fabricated nature of the documentary, and the absence of any corroborating information from governmental or scientific sources. The enduring appeal of 'Alternative 3' lies in its ability to tap into anxieties about environmental collapse, government secrecy, and the potential for advanced technological capabilities beyond public awareness.
subtitle: Hoax documentary sparking conspiracy theories

claims.claim: Secret human colonies exist on Mars.
claims.evidence: Grainy footage in the documentary, coded messages, and alleged witness testimonies.
claims.criticism: Footage and interviews were staged; 'witnesses' were actors. No verifiable evidence supports Martian colonies.

claims.claim: Scientists and other individuals have been secretly recruited or eliminated to maintain the secret.
claims.evidence: Lists of missing scientists presented in the documentary.
claims.criticism: The connection between the missing scientists and the purported conspiracy is unsubstantiated. Many cases have alternative explanations.

claims.claim: Earth faces an imminent environmental catastrophe, necessitating off-world colonization.
claims.evidence: The documentary alludes to undisclosed environmental threats.
claims.criticism: While environmental concerns are valid, the documentary's portrayal of a secret, imminent catastrophe lacks scientific basis and relies on fear-mongering tactics.

keyFigures.name: David Ambrose
keyFigures.role: Author of the novelization of the documentary.

keyFigures.name: James Randi
keyFigures.role: Magician and scientific skeptic who debunked paranormal and conspiracy claims.

timeline.event: David Ambrose publishes the novelization of 'Alternative 3'.
The 'Alternative 3' theory continues to circulate online and in conspiracy circles.

sources.author: David Clarke
sources.work: How UFOs Conquered the World: The History of a Modern Myth
sources.title: David Clarke

sources.author: Andy Roberts
sources.work: UFOs: The Definitive Guide to Unidentified Flying Objects and Related Phenomena
sources.title: Andy Roberts
